title: Should we worry about exposure to parasites?

chunks.title: Introduction to Animal-Based Diet
chunks.summary: The speaker expresses their enthusiasm for an animal-based diet, highlighting their preference for sourcing meat from organs like lungs and hearts, as well as raw dairy products.
chunks.transcript: and i'm eating an animal-based diet and i love it and i when i'm back home i even get the majority of my meat from organs even lung and heart and raw dairy and that's amazing

chunks.title: Concerns About Parasites
chunks.summary: The speaker raises concerns about parasites in the context of an animal-based diet, questioning whether exposure to parasites poses a risk to healthy individuals and discussing how parasites are managed in livestock.
chunks.transcript: my question is regarding parasites so i understand on the one hand that exposure parasites may not be a problem if we are perfectly healthy hypothetically because in nature there's parasites and on the other hands i know from pharma that they are having problems with parasites in their animals specifically with grass fed if there is not chickens or anything coming like in regenerative

chunks.title: Freeze Drying and Safety of Organs
chunks.summary: The discussion shifts to the effectiveness of freeze drying in eliminating parasites and harmful bacteria from desiccated organs, with emphasis on safety and microbiological testing.
chunks.transcript: yes the answer to that the last question is yes absolutely freeze drying desiccation will eradicate any parasites and all the desiccated organs go through microbiology testing before we encapsulate them so there's not going to be any contamination with bacteria so there's a lot of rigorous testing when you're making a supplement so

chunks.title: Raw vs. Cooked Organs
chunks.summary: The speaker discusses the nutritional differences between raw and cooked organs, particularly liver, noting the benefits and drawbacks of each preparation method while addressing concerns about parasites.
chunks.transcript: are they as good as fresh organs no fresh organs are the best whether their coats are raw and so then you're like okay freeze writing is great we see thousands of comments now tens of thousands of comments people who find massive improvements to pre-strike but a lot of people want to get fresh organs too so then you're stuck with okay how do you do fresh organs you do it raw do you do a cooked let's just take liver for instance if you take liver and you cook it and you cook it all the way through does it have a benefit yes but are you going to lose things like riboflavin which are pretty beneficial yeah so

chunks.title: The Role of the Liver
chunks.summary: The speaker elaborates on the liver's function as a biotransformation organ rather than purely a filter, explaining its role in processing toxins and the nutritional richness of liver.
chunks.transcript: dealt with first people think the liver is a filter the liver is not filled with liver is a biotransformation powerhouse that transforms toxins into things that can be excreted in the urine improve so that's what the liver is the liver doesn't accumulate many toxins it will accumulate some heavy metals but it doesn't accumulate like all the toxins and all the pesticides it wants to get rid of things generally speaking and literally useful design and get rid of heavy metals over the course as well it's not just going to store store store in the course of life and then people will think oh it's not why would you eat this filtering organism well this filtering organ well it's because it's so rich and all these nutrients that are hard to find other places so there's a

chunks.title: Evaluating the Safety of Liver
chunks.summary: The speaker provides insights on assessing liver quality for parasites, emphasizing that a smooth appearance is a positive indicator and addressing common fears related to consuming liver.
chunks.transcript: talked to butchers in the past they'll say you can see did you guys see the liver yesterday we got footage of this it was pretty much brown or like dark red and smooth there were no scars i'm pretty sure that a lot of parasites leave scars on the liver they make the liver look not great like you could see it like an experienced pitcher could see it when they're cutting into the liver if your liver is smooth and red and juicy like that's

chunks.title: Common Misconceptions About Parasites
chunks.summary: In the conclusion, the speaker dismisses widespread fears about parasites, suggesting they are less common than perceived and recounting personal experiences with testing for gastrointestinal parasites.
chunks.transcript: happening in the media like you've all got parasites you know i just don't think that many people have parasites i i think that a lot fewer of us have parasites than than we are led to believe people would say oh if you eat any meat that's raw at all you have parasites and that's in my opinion like you just don't see it you don't see it on gi maps you don't see it when you do these things everyone hundreds of gi maps and i've really never seen a worm not once i've seen one amoeba one amoeba and this lady who was really sick and didn't feel good who knows where she got she probably didn't get it for me so i got from water it was entertainment histolytica i've seen one amoeba i've never seen a round worm i've never seen a fluke i've never seen a tapeworm hundreds of gi maps i'm going to say they don't occur but it's it's more rare than we're led to believe i mean people want to you know they want to show these crazy videos on instagram like look at my food it's just like spaghetti of worms like rare you know maybe if you live in africa you're drinking water from you know like an after watering hole maybe but it's
